notes: Official Brooklyn Robot Foundry North Brooklyn / Southern Queens franchise (Bed-Stuy hub). Program ages, offerings, service area, and local contact email come from the Bed-Stuy welcome and programs pages; national phone and contact email from the main contact page. Hero image is the franchise welcome page primaryImageOfPage JPEG on brooklynrobotfoundry.com, uploaded via POST /api/ingest/upload.
missingOrUncertain: pricePerClass is 0: after-school and in-school enrichment are semester or school-contract programs without a published per-class rate on the cited pages; Foundry Fundays full-day registration on the shop lists session prices (e.g. $75–$190) that vary by date and location.

No single public storefront street address is published for this franchise; programs run at schools, partner sites, and customer-supplied party venues across the listed service area. Address field uses the Bed-Stuy hub ZIP (11206) called out on registration flows plus the official service-area label.
rating and reviewCount are not published on the official program pages; both set to 0.
dayTimeTags are inferred from after-school, school-holiday Fundays, and weekend party offerings; exact weekly times depend on each school or registered session.

document.name: Brooklyn Robot Foundry — North Brooklyn STEAM Classes
document.address: North Brooklyn & Southern Queens service area, Bedford-Stuyvesant, Brooklyn, NY 11206
document.shortDescription: Hands-on, screen-free robot-building STEAM classes for pre-K through 8th grade across North Brooklyn and southern Queens — after-school residencies, school-holiday Fundays, parties, and family workshops.
document.longDescription:
Brooklyn Robot Foundry’s North Brooklyn / Southern Queens franchise (Bed-Stuy hub) brings screen-free, hands-on robot-building programs to families across Greenpoint, Williamsburg, Bushwick, Bedford-Stuyvesant, Ridgewood, and nearby Queens neighborhoods. The official Bed-Stuy welcome page describes age-appropriate classes from pre-K through 8th grade, plus family and adult events, delivered through after-school residencies, in-school enrichment, school-holiday Foundry Fundays, Summer Circuits camps, birthday parties, and scout or youth-group workshops.

The franchise programs page for this location lists semester-style After-School Classes and In-School Enrichment for children roughly ages 2–14, single-day Foundry Fundays for grades K–5 when school is closed, full-week Summer Circuits, Family Fun sessions for ages 2–12 with a grown-up, and mobile parties where families supply the venue. The locations directory titles this territory “North Brooklyn & Southern Queens (Bed-Stuy Area).” Local inquiries are directed to caroline@brooklynrobotfoundry.com on the welcome page; the national contact page lists phone (347) 762-6840 and contact@brooklynrobotfoundry.com.

Need-based scholarships for select programs are linked from the Bed-Stuy site. This listing image is the official welcome-page photograph of children working on a robot project, re-hosted on ImgBB for ClassScout storage policy compliance.

Sources: https://brooklynrobotfoundry.com/bed-stuy/welcome/ https://brooklynrobotfoundry.com/programs/?location=15 https://brooklynrobotfoundry.com/locations/ https://brooklynrobotfoundry.com/contact/
document.announcementDescription: Bed-Stuy franchise page lists registration open for 2026 Summer Circuits and school-holiday Foundry Fundays — see brooklynrobotfoundry.com for dates.
